subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities recorded as base ten numerals as well as rounding these to given decimal places and significant figures.
indicator_description: Model number quantities more than 1,000,000,000 using graph sheets, isometric papers and multi-base blocks
examplars:
E.g., 1. Model number quantities up to 1,000,000,000 (one billion) using graph sheets or multi-base ten materials...
E.g.2. Use multiples of 10s, 50s, 100s and 200s to represent numbers in multiple of ways (make sure each figure is used)
E.g.3. Use tokens (or paper-made currency notes) such as GHC20, GHC50, GHC100 and GHC200 to work out how many of each denomination would be required to model given amount up to one billion.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities recorded as base ten numerals as well as rounding these to given decimal places and significant figures.
indicator_description: Compare and order whole numbers more than 1,000,000,000 and represent the comparison using ">, <, or ="
examplars:
E.g. 1.Skip count forwards and backwards in 25s, 50s and 250s beginning from 1000.
E.g.2. Identify numbers which are for instance, 500,000 more than or less than a given 8-digit or 9-digit number.
E.g.3. Use phrases such as "is equal to", "is greater than" and "is less than" as well as their symbols such as ">", "<" and "=" to compare any two numbers.
E.g.4. Identify, read and write numbers in given positions in a number chart.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities recorded as base ten numerals as well as rounding these to given decimal places and significant figures.
indicator_description: Round (off, up, down) whole numbers more than 1,000,000,000 to the nearest hundred-thousand, ten-thousands, thousands, hundreds and tens
examplars:
E.g. 1. Round off whole numbers up to over 1,000,000,000 to the nearest hundred-thousands, ten-thousands, thousands, hundreds, etc.
E.g. 2. Explain the differences between the "round up" and "round down" concepts.
E.g.3. Express whole numbers to significant figures

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities recorded as base ten numerals as well as rounding these to given decimal places and significant figures.
indicator_description: Round decimals to the nearest tenth, hundredth, thousandths, etc.
examplars: E.g.1 Round (off, up and down) decimals to the nearest tenths, hundredths, thousandths..

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities recorded as base ten numerals as well as rounding these to given decimal places and significant figures.
indicator_description: Express decimal numerals to given significant and decimal places
examplars:
E.g.1 Explain when zero (0) is significant in a decimal numeral
E.g.2. Round the following numbers to 3sf, 4sf, 6sf
E.g.3. Express decimal numbers to a given number of decimal places

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.2.1 Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties used to solve problems
indicator_description: Multiply and divide given numbers by powers of 10 including decimals and benchmark fractions
examplars:
E.g.1. Recall multiplication facts up to 144 and related division facts.
E.g.2. Recall decimal names of given benchmark fractions converted to decimals or percentages (and vice versa)
E.g. 3. Find the product of a given decimal number when it is multiplied by 10, 100, 1000, 1/10, 1/100, 1/1000 etc.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.2.1 Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties used to solve problems
indicator_description: Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties used to perform calculations.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Apply the halving and doubling techniques to determine the product of two given numbers.
E.g. 2. Apply the distributive property to determine the product of two given numbers

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.2.1 Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties used to solve problems
indicator_description: Apply mental mathematics strategies to solve word problems.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Play mental mathematics games: - learners use mental strategies to perform the following addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
E.g.2. Play mental mathematics games solving contextual word problems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.7.1.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of (i) whole numbers, and (ii) decimal numbers, to solve problems.
indicator_description: Add and subtract up to four-digit numbers.
examplars: E.g. 1. Use partitioning (or expanded form) and place value system to add and subtract whole and decimal numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.7.1.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of (i) whole numbers, and (ii) decimal numbers, to solve problems.
indicator_description: Multiply or divide multi-digit numbers by 1- and 2- digit numbers
examplars:
E.g.1 Use partitioning/expanded form to multiply and divide efficiently
E.g.2. Multiply whole numbers using the vertical place value method or lattice method
E.g.3 Use the distributive property to multiply
E.g.4 Investigate and determine basic division facts including divisibility test.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.7.1.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of (i) whole numbers, and (ii) decimal numbers, to solve problems.
indicator_description: Create and solve story problems involving decimals on the four basic operations.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Solve word problems involving operations on whole numbers and decimals.
E.g.2 Solve word problems on data presented in a table

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.2.3 Demonstrate understanding and the use of powers of natural numbers in solving problems.
indicator_description: Illustrate with examples the meaning of repeated factors using counting objects such as bottle tops or bundle sticks.
examplars:
E.g.1: Model repeated factors using counters or bottle tops.
E.g.2 Explain what is meant by a power of a number.
E.g.3 Explain the features of a power.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.2.3 Demonstrate understanding and the use of powers of natural numbers in solving problems.
indicator_description: Express a given number as a product of a given number or numbers, as well as, in the form of a power or two such numbers as product of powers
examplars: E.g. 1 Express numbers such as 32, 81, 49 as products of powers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.2.3 Demonstrate understanding and the use of powers of natural numbers in solving problems.
indicator_description: Show that the value of any natural number with zero as its exponent or index is 1 and use it to solve problems.
examplars: E.g.1 Verify why the value of any natural number with exponent zero is 1.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.2.3 Demonstrate understanding and the use of powers of natural numbers in solving problems.
indicator_description: Find the value of a number written in index form.
examplars: E.g.1 Calculate values of numbers like 5 cubed, 3 to the power of 4, etc.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.2.3 Demonstrate understanding and the use of powers of natural numbers in solving problems.
indicator_description: Apply the concept of powers of numbers (product of prime) to find Highest Common Factor (HCF).
examplars:
E.g.1 Expand a given number using product of prime concept to find HCF.
E.g. 2 Solve HCF Real life examples.
E.g. 3 Solve LCM Real life examples.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.3.1 Simplify, compare and order a mixture of positive fractions (i.e. common, percent and decimal) by changing all to equivalent (i) fractions (ii) decimals, or (iii) percentages.
indicator_description: Determine and recall the percentages and decimals of given benchmark fractions (i.e. tenths, fifths, fourths, thirds and halves) and use these to compare quantities.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Review the concept of fractions.
E.g. 2. Work out common, and decimal fractions and percent equivalences of given benchmark fractions to complete a table.
E.g. 3. Identifying fractions which are (i) closer to half; (ii) closer to one; and (iii) closer to zero in games with fraction cards and fraction wheel.
E.g. 4. Simplify, compare and order common fractions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.3.1 Simplify, compare and order a mixture of positive fractions (i.e. common, percent and decimal) by changing all to equivalent (i) fractions (ii) decimals, or (iii) percentages.
indicator_description: Compare and order fractions (i.e. common, percent and decimal fractions up to thousandths) limit to the benchmark fractions.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Arrange in descending order given fractions.
E.g. 2. Find which decimal fractions is greater.
E.g. 3. Order the decimal numbers from least to greatest.
E.g. 4. Compare and order common and decimal fractions and percent, and express them in one form.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.3.2 Demonstrate an understanding of the process of addition and/or subtraction of fractions and apply this in solving problems
indicator_description: Explain the process of addition and subtraction of two or three unlike and mixed fractions.
examplars:
E.g. 1. To add mixed fractions, we first add the whole numbers and then add the fractions.
E.g. 2. To subtract mixed fractions, we first subtract the whole numbers and then subtract the fractions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.3.2 Demonstrate an understanding of the process of addition and/or subtraction of fractions and apply this in solving problems
indicator_description: Solve problems involving addition or subtraction of fractions.
examplars: E.g. 1. Solve word problems involving addition or subtraction of fractions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.3.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the process of multiplying and dividing positive fractions and apply this in solving problems
indicator_description: Explain the process of multiplying a fraction (i.e. common, percent and decimal fractions up to thousandths) by a whole number and by a fraction.
examplars:
E.g. 1. To multiply a whole number by a fraction, the multiplication is read as 'times'.
E.g. 2. To multiply a fraction by a whole number, the multiplication is read as 'of'.
E.g. 3. Calculate percentages of given numbers.
E.g. 4. To multiply a fraction by a fraction, the multiplication is read as 'of'.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.3.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the process of multiplying and dividing positive fractions and apply this in solving problems
indicator_description: Find a fraction of given quantity (i.e. money or given quantity of objects)
examplars:
E.g. 1. To multiply a given quantity by a fraction is just like multiplying by a whole number, so the multiplication is read as 'of'.
E.g. 2. Solve word problems involving fractions of quantities.
E.g. 3. Solve problems finding fractions based on graph data.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.3.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the process of multiplying and dividing positive fractions and apply this in solving problems
indicator_description: Explain the process of dividing a fraction (i.e. common, percent and decimal fractions up to thousandths) by a 1-digit whole number and by a fraction.
examplars: E.g. 1. To divide a whole number by a fraction, the division means 'how many times the fraction goes into the whole number' and multiply dividend by reciprocal of divisor.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.3.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the process of multiplying and dividing positive fractions and apply this in solving problems
indicator_description: Determine the result of dividing a quantity (i.e. money or objects) or a fraction by a fraction
examplars:
E.g. 1. Solve word problems dividing quantities by fractions.
E.g. 2. Solve volume division problems.
E.g. 3. Interpret graphs and solve quantities.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.4.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of ratios and its relationship to fractions and use it to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning
indicator_description: Find ratio and use ratio language to describe relationship between two quantities.
examplars:
E.g. 1 Determine ratio of given quantities.
E.g.2 Express two quantities as a ratio.
E.g.3 Describe quantities with ratio language.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.4.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of ratios and its relationship to fractions and use it to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning
indicator_description: Use the concept of a unit rate associated with a ratio a:b with b≠0, and use rate language in the context of a ratio relationship.
examplars:
E.g.1 Write given ratios as unit rate.
E.g.2 Work out rates and use them in solving problems.
E.g.3 Use tables/diagrams to explain the concept of rate that compares two different quantities measured in different units.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.4.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of ratios and its relationship to fractions and use it to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning
indicator_description: Make tables of equivalent ratios (written as common fractions) relating quantities that are proportional.
examplars: E.g.1 Shared an amount of money in the ratio of ages and find the total amount shared using equivalent ratios table.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.4.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of ratios and its relationship to fractions and use it to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning
indicator_description: Use the proportional reasoning to find missing values in the tables, and plot pairs of values on the coordinate plane.
examplars: E.g. 2 Find the missing value marked x in a table of equivalent ratios.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.1.4.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of ratios and its relationship to fractions and use it to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning
indicator_description: Find a percent of a quantity as a rate per 100 (e.g. 30% of a quantity means 30/100 times the quantity).
examplars: E.g.1 Calculate commission, discount amount, sale price, and interest rates for given quantities.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Derive the rule for a set of points of a relation, draw a table of values to graph the relation in a number plane and make predictions about subsequent elements of the relation.
indicator_description: Extend a given relation presented with and without symbolic materials and explain how each element differs from the preceding one.
examplars:
E.g.1 Extend a given symbolic relation.
E.g.2 Study the pattern of numbers and complete table.
E.g.3 Extend a given number relation.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Derive the rule for a set of points of a relation, draw a table of values to graph the relation in a number plane and make predictions about subsequent elements of the relation.
indicator_description: Describe the rule for a given relation using mathematical language such as one more, one less, one more than twice, etc.
examplars:
E.g.1 Describe given relations (e.g., "Is the square of", "is a double of").
E.g.2 Describe the rule for a relation using mathematics language from a table pattern.
E.g.3 State the rules in words to represent a given relation.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Derive the rule for a set of points of a relation, draw a table of values to graph the relation in a number plane and make predictions about subsequent elements of the relation.
indicator_description: Identify the relation or rule in a pattern/mapping presented numerically or symbolically and predict subsequent elements
examplars:
E.g.1 Determine the rule for a given symbolic pattern.
E.g.2 Determine the rule for a given numerical pattern.
E.g.3 Determine an element when given the rule.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Derive the rule for a set of points of a relation, draw a table of values to graph the relation in a number plane and make predictions about subsequent elements of the relation.
indicator_description: Locate points on the number plane, draw a table of values of a given relation, draw graphs for given relations and use them to solve problems.
examplars:
E.g.1 Make a table of values for a given rule.
E.g.2 Locate points on the number plane.
E.g.3 Draw graphs for given relations.
E.g.4 Use knowledge of identifying and plotting points in a number plane to solve problems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Simplify algebraic expressions involving the four basic operations and substituting values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
indicator_description: Create simple algebraic expressions using simple logic to translate a set of instructions into an algebraic expression.
examplars:
E.g.1 Form algebraic expressions for given mathematical statements.
E.g.2 Form algebraic expressions from real life situations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Simplify algebraic expressions involving the four basic operations and substituting values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
indicator_description: Perform addition and subtraction of algebraic expressions with rational coefficients.
examplars:
E.g.1 Add algebraic expressions.
E.g.2 Subtract algebraic expressions.
E.g.3 Adding and subtracting algebraic expressions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Simplify algebraic expressions involving the four basic operations and substituting values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
indicator_description: Perform multiplication and division of algebraic expressions with rational coefficients.
examplars:
E.g.1 Solve multiplication of algebraic expressions.
E.g.2 Solve division of algebraic expressions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Simplify algebraic expressions involving the four basic operations and substituting values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
indicator_description: Substitute values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
examplars:
E.g.1 Simplify the following expressions and substitute the values to evaluate them.
E.g.2 Simplify the following expressions and substitute the values to evaluate them.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Simplify algebraic expressions involving the four basic operations and substituting values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
indicator_description: Use properties of the four operations to simplify algebraic expressions with rational coefficients.
examplars: E.g.1 Simplify algebraic expressions involving the four operations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of linear equations of the form x + a = b (where a and b are integers) by modelling problems as a linear equation and solving the problems concretely, pictorially, and symbolically.
indicator_description: Translate word problems to linear equations in one variable and vice versa
examplars:
E.g.1: Use a flag diagram for equations and their inverses to solve equations.
E.g.2: Translate word problems to linear equations.
E.g.3: Write word problems for given linear equations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of linear equations of the form x + a = b (where a and b are integers) by modelling problems as a linear equation and solving the problems concretely, pictorially, and symbolically.
indicator_description: Model and solve linear equations using concrete materials (e.g., counters and integer tiles) and describe the process orally and symbolically.
examplars:
E.g. 1 Model and solve linear equations set with objects on a balance.
E.g. 2. Model and solve linear equations set with shapes on a balance.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of linear equations of the form x + a = b (where a and b are integers) by modelling problems as a linear equation and solving the problems concretely, pictorially, and symbolically.
indicator_description: Model linear equations, then write mathematical expressions and describe the process of solving the equation using algebraic tiles.
examplars:
E.g.1 Model and describe process with algebraic tiles.
E.g.2 Add and simplify using tiles model.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.2.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of linear equations of the form x + a = b (where a and b are integers) by modelling problems as a linear equation and solving the problems concretely, pictorially, and symbolically.
indicator_description: Solve linear equations in one variable
examplars: E.g. 1 Using the idea of balance solve simple linear equations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of angles including adjacent, vertically opposite, complementary, supplementary and use them to solve problems.
indicator_description: Measure and classify angles according to their measured sizes - right, acute, obtuse and reflex.
examplars:
E.g. 1 Sort angles into those which are right, acute, obtuse or reflex angles from photocopied worksheets with several angles to measure.
E.g. 2 Use a protractor to draw angles.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of angles including adjacent, vertically opposite, complementary, supplementary and use them to solve problems.
indicator_description: Apply the fact that (i) complementary angles are two angles that have a sum of 90°, and (ii) supplementary angles are two angles that have a sum of 180° to solve problems.
examplars:
E.g.1 Determine the missing angle marked x for complementary angles.
E.g.2 Determine the missing angle marked x for supplementary angles.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of angles including adjacent, vertically opposite, complementary, supplementary and use them to solve problems.
indicator_description: Use adjacent, supplementary and vertically opposite angles to solve problems
examplars:
E.g. 1 Determine the angle(s) marked with letters in the adjacent and/or supplementary angles below.
E.g.2 Identify each pair of angles as adjacent, vertically opposite, complementary or supplementary.
E.g.3 Use the figure to identify and label angles.
E.g. 4 Use adjacent, vertically opposite, complementary or supplementary to solve problems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.1.2 Demonstrate how to construct a perpendicular to a line from a given point, bisect a line, bisect angles, and construct angles of the following sizes: 30°, 45°, 60°, 75° and 90°
indicator_description: Construct a line segment perpendicular to another line segment.
examplars:
E.g.1: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct a copy of a given line segment.
E.g.2: Use a pair of compasses and ruler to construct a perpendicular at a point on a line segment, and drop a perpendicular from a given point outside a line segment.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.1.2 Demonstrate how to construct a perpendicular to a line from a given point, bisect a line, bisect angles, and construct angles of the following sizes: 30°, 45°, 60°, 75° and 90°
indicator_description: Construct the perpendicular bisector of a line segment
examplars:
E.g.1: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct a perpendicular bisector of a given line segment.
E.g. 2: Draw and bisect given lines.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.1.2 Demonstrate how to construct a perpendicular to a line from a given point, bisect a line, bisect angles, and construct angles of the following sizes: 30°, 45°, 60°, 75° and 90°
indicator_description: Copy and bisect angles
examplars:
E.g.1: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to copy a given angle.
E.g.2 Perform geometric construction to bisect a given angle.
E.g. 3 Identify which of the angles has a correct angle bisector.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.1.2 Demonstrate how to construct a perpendicular to a line from a given point, bisect a line, bisect angles, and construct angles of the following sizes: 30°, 45°, 60°, 75° and 90°
indicator_description: Construct angles of 90° and 45°
examplars:
E.g. 1: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct an angle of 90°.
E.g.2: Construct an angle of 45° by bisecting an angle of 90°.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.1.2 Demonstrate how to construct a perpendicular to a line from a given point, bisect a line, bisect angles, and construct angles of the following sizes: 30°, 45°, 60°, 75° and 90°
indicator_description: Construct angles of 60° and 30°
examplars:
E.g.1: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct an angle of 60°.
E.g.2 Construct an angle of 30° by bisecting an angle whose measure is 60°.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.1.2 Demonstrate how to construct a perpendicular to a line from a given point, bisect a line, bisect angles, and construct angles of the following sizes: 30°, 45°, 60°, 75° and 90°
indicator_description: Construct angles whose measures 15° and 75°.
examplars:
E.g.1: Construct an angle of 15° by bisecting an angle of 30°.
E.g.2 Construct angles like 7.5°, 60°, 30°, 15°.
E.g.3: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct an angle of 75°.
E.g.4: Construct and bisect 75°.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.1.2 Demonstrate how to construct a perpendicular to a line from a given point, bisect a line, bisect angles, and construct angles of the following sizes: 30°, 45°, 60°, 75° and 90°
indicator_description: Describe examples of perpendicular line segments, perpendicular bisectors and angle bisectors in the environment.
examplars:
E.g.1: Identify angle bisectors and perpendicular bisectors in structures and artefacts in the environment.
E.g.2 Estimate the measure of the size of angles in artefacts, tools, and structures.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.7.3.2.1 Demonstrate the ability to find the perimeter of plane shapes including circles using the concept of pi (π) to find the circumference of a circle.
indicator_description: Calculate the perimeter of given shapes whose dimensions are in two units (i.e. cm and mm, m and cm, or km and m)
examplars:
E.g. 1. Calculate the perimeter of a shape with dimensions given in km and m by converting to the smaller unit.
E.g. 2 Calculate the perimeter of a shape with dimensions given in cm and mm by converting to decimal fractions.
E.g. 3 Calculate the perimeter of a shape with dimensions given in m and cm by converting to decimal fractions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.7.3.2.1 Demonstrate the ability to find the perimeter of plane shapes including circles using the concept of pi (π) to find the circumference of a circle.
indicator_description: Use the relationships between the diameter and the circumference to deduce the formula for finding the circumference of a circle and use it to solve problems.
examplars:
E.g.1: Identify the name the parts of a circle radius, diameter, circumference, arc, sector, etc.
E.g.2: Measure the radius, diameter and circumference of circular objects.
E.g.3: Explain the relationship between the diameter and circumference of a circle.
E.g.4: Use the relationship between the diameter and circumference of a circle to solve problems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.7.3.2.1 Demonstrate the ability to find the perimeter of plane shapes including circles using the concept of pi (π) to find the circumference of a circle.
indicator_description: Draw in a square grid rectangles and triangles with given dimensions.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Draw rectangles with given scaled dimensions on a grid.
E.g. 2. Draw in the dot square grid another triangle whose area is 3 square units.
E.g. 3. Find area of triangle in grid and draw different triangles with the same area.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.7.3.2.2 Derive the formula for determining the area of a triangle and use it to solve problems
indicator_description: Use the relationships between a triangle and a rectangle (or parallelogram) to deduce the formula for determining the area of a triangle.
examplars:
E.g.1: Determine the number of unit squares enclosed by the triangles.
E.g.2: Spot the RECTANGLE enclosing the triangles to find the unit squares in each triangle.
E.g.3: Spot the Parallelogram from which the triangle was formed.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.7.3.2.2 Derive the formula for determining the area of a triangle and use it to solve problems.
indicator_description: Determine the area of a triangle.
examplars: E.g. 1. Calculate the area of the triangles.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.2.3 Demonstrate understanding of bearings, vector and its components using real life cases.
indicator_description: Describe the bearing of a point from another point
examplars:
E.g.1 use protractor to find the marked angles. For each diagram write the three-digit bearing.
E.g.2 Recognise true bearings as the angle measured in the clockwise direction from the North.
E.g.3 Express the following vectors graphically and measure each angle.
E.g.4 Draw the following vectors and measure each angle.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.2.3 Demonstrate understanding of bearings, vector and its components using real life cases.
indicator_description: Explain how to find the back bearing when the direction of travel has a bearing which is less than 180° and/ or greater than 180°.
examplars:
E.g.1 For each question below find: The bearing of B from A; The bearing of A from B
E.g.2 The bearing of P from Q is 060°. What is the bearing of Q from P
E.g.3 The bearing of P from Q is 145°. What is the bearing of Q from P

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.2.3 Demonstrate understanding of bearings, vector and its components using real life cases.
indicator_description: Distinguish between scalar and vector quantities
examplars:
E.g.1 Read on scalar quantity and vector quantity on the internet.
E.g.2 Group these examples under scalar quantity and vector quantity.
E.g.3 Identify a vector as a movement (distance) along a given bearing
E.g.4 Draw a vector given its length and bearing.
E.g.5 Identify the distance along a vector as its magnitude and the 3 - digit clockwise angle from the north as its bearing
E.g.6 Identify a zero vector as a point with no magnitude and direction.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.2.3 Demonstrate understanding of bearings, vector and its components using real life cases.
indicator_description: Represent vector in the column (component) form and determine its magnitude and direction.
examplars:
E.g. 1 Write each of the following as column vectors using graph.
E.g.2 Use any other method apart from graph to write the following as column vectors and find it magnitude and direction.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.2.3 Demonstrate understanding of bearings, vector and its components using real life cases.
indicator_description: Convert vectors in the column (component) form to the Magnitude–Bearing form and vice versa
examplars:
E.g.1 Use the Pythagorean theorem to find the length or the magnitude of a vector.
Eg.2 Find the magnitude and the direction of the following vectors

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.3.1 Perform a single transformation (i.e. reflection and translation) on a 2D shape using graph paper (including technology) and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence, similarity, etc.)
indicator_description: Determine shapes in real life that have reflectional (or fold) symmetries.
examplars:
E.g. 1: Identify examples of designs (or objects) in everyday life that have reflectional (or fold) symmetries.
E.g. 2: In how many different ways can one more square be shaded in this shape so that it can have a line of symmetry?

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.3.1 Perform a single transformation (i.e. reflection and translation) on a 2D shape using graph paper (including technology) and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence, similarity, etc.)
indicator_description: Plot points and shapes (i.e. plane figures) on a coordinate plane and draw their images under reflection in given lines
examplars:
E.g. 1: Plot points and shapes with given coordinates in the number plane.
E.g. 2: Identify points with given coordinates and lines in the number plane.
E.g. 3: Plot given points (or shape) the number plane and draw its images under reflection in (i) the x-axis, (ii) y-axis and (iii) y=x
E.g. 4: Derive the coordinate rules.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.3.1 Perform a single transformation (i.e. reflection and translation) on a 2D shape using graph paper (including technology) and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence, similarity, etc.)
indicator_description: Plot points and shapes (i.e. plane figures) on a coordinate plane and draw their images under translation by a given vector.
examplars:
E.g. 1: As people go down a slide, they undergo a translation.
E.g. 2: Plot point(s) and shapes (i.e. plane figures) in a coordinate plane using a translating vector and describe the changes in the vertices as well as the direction of the movement.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.3.3.1 Perform a single transformation (i.e. reflection and translation) on a 2D shape using graph paper (including technology) and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence, similarity, etc.)
indicator_description: Verify the concept of congruent and similar shapes in coordinate plane using properties of both the object(s) and image(s); and in real life situations (carpet designs, fabric pattern)
examplars:
E.g. 1: Verify which shapes are similar and which are congruent.
E.g. 2. Which of the following shapes are congruent?

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.4.1.1 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods to collect data (quantitative and qualitative), display and analyse the data (grouped/ungrouped) presented in frequency tables, line graphs, pie graphs, bar graphs or pictographs and use these to solve and/or pose problems.
indicator_description: Select and justify a method to collect data (quantitative and qualitative) to answer a given question.
examplars:
E.g. 1. In small groups, learners discuss and write down how they would make decisions in the following situations, what facts they would take into account and how they would collect these ‘facts’.
E.g. 2. Lead a discussion on the methods of data collection below and ask them to identify which method they will use to gather the facts for each situation.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.4.1.1 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods to collect data (quantitative and qualitative), display and analyse the data (grouped/ungrouped) presented in frequency tables, line graphs, pie graphs, bar graphs or pictographs and use these to solve and/or pose problems.
indicator_description: Design and administer a questionnaire for collecting data to answer questions and record the results.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Conduct a survey (within a small group of learners) by producing a question form and collecting real information.
E.g. 2. Use a table to organise the data obtained with the question form (or questionnaire).

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.4.1.1 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods to collect data (quantitative and qualitative), display and analyse the data (grouped/ungrouped) presented in frequency tables, line graphs, pie graphs, bar graphs or pictographs and use these to solve and/or pose problems.
indicator_description: Organise and present data from a survey into a table and/or chart, and analyse it to solve and/or pose problems.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Use tallies to organise into a frequency table marks obtained in a mathematics test by students in a class.
E.g. 2 Use tallies to organise into a frequency table, the data obtained by a group of learners for the number of people living in households around their houses.
E.g. 3. Draw a graph or chart for data organised in a frequency table and use it to answer and/or pose questions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.4.1.2 Determine the measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) for a given ungrouped data and use it to solve problems
indicator_description: Calculate the mean for a given ungrouped data and use it to solve problems
examplars:
E.g. 1 Find the mean for a data set by dividing the sum of all the items in the data set by the by the number of items.
E.g. Find the mean for a data set (in a frequency table) by dividing the sum of all the items in the data set by the by the number of items.
E.g. 3 Solve problems involving calculating the mean or average.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.4.1.2 Determine the measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) for a given ungrouped data and use it to solve problems
indicator_description: Calculate the median for a given ungrouped data and use it to solve problems
examplars:
E.g. 1 Find the median for a data set by arranging the items in the set in an array and identifying the middle item.
E.g. 2 Find the median for a data set (in a frequency table).

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.4.2.1 Identify the sample space for a probability experiment involving single events and express the probabilities of given events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios to solve problems
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding of likelihood of a single outcome occurring by providing examples of events that are impossible, possible, or certain from personal contexts.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Describe each outcome using words like: impossible, possible, or certain.
E.g. 2. Ask learners to work in groups to discuss the outcome of the following events using words like: impossible, possible, or certain

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.4.2.1 Identify the sample space for a probability experiment involving single events and express the probabilities of given events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios to solve problems
indicator_description: Classify the likelihood of a single outcome occurring in a probability experiment as impossible, possible, or certain
examplars:
E.g. 1 Discuss the following outcomes of throwing a die using words like impossible, possible, or certain.
E.g. 2 Discuss the following outcomes of throwing two dice using words like impossible, possible, or certain.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B7.4.2.1 Identify the sample space for a probability experiment involving single events and express the probabilities of given events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios to solve problems
indicator_description: Calculate the probability of the event and express the probability as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios.
examplars: E.g. 1 Calculate the probabilities and complete the table for probability with a single die.
